program_outcomes: Matematik, fen ve mühendislik bilgilerini uygulama becerisi
Deney tasarlama, deney yapma, deney sonuçlarını analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi

Ekonomik, çevresel, sosyal, politik ahlaki, sağlık ve güvenlik, üretilebilirlik ve sürdürülebilirlik gibi gerçekçi gereksinimleri karşılayacak biçimde bir sistemi, parçayı ya da süreci tasarlama becerisi
Mühendislik problemlerini saptama,tanımlama ve çözme becerisi
Mühendislik çözümlerinin, evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda etkilerini anlamak için gerekli genişlikte eğitim
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci ve bunu gerçekleştirebilme becerisi
Mühendislik uygulamaları için gerekli olan teknikleri ve modern araçları kullanma becerisi

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve Inkılap Tarihi I
courses.purpose: Öğrencilerin Atatürk İlke ve İnkılaplarının dayandığı esasları anlayıp, yorum ve analiz yapabilmelerini sağlamak. Üniversite öğrencilerine Türkiye'nin yakın tarihini öğretmek.
courses.description: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün ilke ve inkılapları ışığında Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin kuruluş tarihini temel tanımları, kavramları ve olayları 16. yüzyıl ve 20. yüzyıl çerçevesinde ele alınır.
learning_outcomes: Ülkemizin geçmiş, bugünü ve geçmişi arasında bağ kurmak.
Tarihsel olaylar arasındaki ilişkiyi anlamak
Edinilen bilgi birikimi ile güncel konular hakkında bilgi sahibi olmak.
Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin tarihi mirası hakkında bir görüş oluşturmak.
Milli ve evrensel değerlerin sentezini yapabilme yeteneğinin kazanılması
Derste kazandıkları bilinci akademik ve/veya kişisel hayatlarında kullanmaları
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi dersi ile ilgili temel kavramlar hakkında bilgi verilmesi, tarih, tarih bilimi ve Osmanlı- Türkiye tarihine yönelik yeni yaklaşımlar ve Türk İnkılâbının stratejisi.
Hafta 2: Osmanlı Devleti'nin yıkılışını ve Türk İnkılâbını hazırlayan sebeplere toplu bir bakış (İç sebepler, Dış sebepler, Osmanlı Devleti'nin jeopolitik ve ekonomik durumu)
Hafta 3: XIX. yüzyılda Osmanlı Devleti'nde yenileşme hareketleri (Tanzimat Dönemi, I. Meşrutiyet ve II. Meşrutiyet)

Hafta 4: Osmanlı Devleti'nin Dağılması sürecinde meydana gelen iç ve dış olaylar (Kırım Savaşı, 1877-78 Osmanlı-Rus Savaşı, Makedonya meselesi, 31 Mart olayı, Girit ve Bosna-Hersek'in elden çıkışı, Trablusgarp Savaşı, Balkan Savaşları
Hafta 5: Osmanlı Devleti'nin son dönemindeki fikir akımları (Osmanlıcılık, İslamcılık, Türkçülük, Batıcılık, Sosyalizm) ve Meşrutiyet Devrinde Osmanlı Devleti
Hafta 6: I. Dünya Savaşı (Savaşın çıkışı, Osmanlı Devleti'nin savaşa dâhil oluşu, cepheler ve savaşın sonu) ve Mondros Mütarekesinin imzalanması

Hafta 7: İşgaller, Tepkiler (Kuva-yı Milliyenin ortaya çıkışı), Cemiyetler (Millî, Millî varlığa düşman ve azınlık cemiyetleri), Mondros Mütarekesinden sonra Mustafa Kemal Paşa'nın faaliyetleri ve Anadolu'ya geçmesi

Hafta 9: Millî Mücadele için ilk adım ve Kongreler yoluyla teşkilatlanma (Amasya Genelgesi, Erzurum, Sivas Kongresi ve Batı Anadolu Kongreleri), Son Osmanlı Meclis-i Mebusanı'nın toplanması, Misak-ı Millî'nin kabulü ve İstanbul'un işgali
Hafta 10: Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi'nin açılışı, Meclisin yapısı, çıkardığı yasalar ve faaliyetleri, Meclisin açılışına iç ve dış tepkiler
Hafta 11: Sevr Barış Anlaşması; Sevr Anlaşması'nda bugüne yönelik tehditler, Doğu ve Güney cephelerindeki durum
Hafta 12: Düzenli ordunun kuruluşu; Kuva-yı Milliye'nin tasfiyesi, Yunan genel taarruzu ve Batı Cephesi'ndeki savaşlar

Hafta 13: Mustafa Kemal Paşa'nın Başkomutanlığı, Tekâlif-i Milliye emirleri, Sakarya Savaşı ve sonrasındaki dış politika gelişmeleri (Türk-Rus, Türk-Afgan münasebetleri, Londra Konferansı, Ankara İtilafnamesi)

Hafta 14: Büyük Taarruz ve Mudanya Mütarekesi'nin imzalanması, Lozan konferansı öncesindeki gelişmeler, Lozan konferansı öncesindeki gelişmeler ve Konferansın toplanması; Barış anlaşmasının imzalanması, Saltanatın kaldırılması, Cumhuriyetin İlanı ve gelişmeler, Saltanatın kaldırılması, Cumhuriyetin İlanı ve gelişmeler.

courses.name: Computer Programming
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide a good understanding of the Python programming language. Students will learn the solutions needed to build programs that can interact with all kinds of input in order to produce correct outputs. They will be well prepared for advanced Python programming to be covered in the course.
courses.description: This course is an introduction to programming. It covers the fundamentals of developing algorithms to solve various problems. The course includes basic data types, control mechanisms such as conditional statements and loops, arrays, and input/output topics.
learning_outcomes: Be able to program in Python at an intermediate level
Understand and use input/output and data structures
Understand and apply modules and functions, and use libraries
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Variables, Data Types and Expressions

courses.name: General Chemistry
courses.purpose: To gain basic chemistry knowledge such as the properties of matter, its measurement, structure, the forces holding the structure together, chemical reactions, naming, and classification, and to be able to combine this knowledge with everyday applications.
courses.description: Properties and classification of matter, measurement of matter, uncertainty in the scientific method, significant figures; atoms, molecules, ions; atomic theories; formulation and nomenclature; chemical reactions; calculations related to reactions, molecular mass, mole concept; solution chemistry; gases; thermochemistry, heat and work concepts; quantum theory; electronic configuration, periodicity; chemical bonds and secondary bonds.
learning_outcomes: To have knowledge about atomic and molecular structures
To have knowledge about molecular bonding from simple to complex
To be able to perform chemical calculations
To have knowledge about the properties of gases and solutions
To have knowledge about the relationship between heat and chemical reactions
To know the basic principles of quantum chemistry
To gain the ability to relate chemistry knowledge to everyday applications
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Properties and classification of matter, measurement of matter
Hafta 2: Uncertainty in the scientific method, significant figures
Hafta 3: Structure of the atom and atomic theories
Hafta 6: Chemical reactions in aqueous solution
Hafta 10: Periodic table and atomic properties
Hafta 14: Preparation for the final exam
resources: Lecture notes and presentations
Petrucci R.H., Harwood W.S., Herring F.G., General Chemistry

courses.name: Engineering English I
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students basic engineering terminology and skills.
courses.description: This course aims to develop language skills related to engineering English.
learning_outcomes: Develop critical thinking and reasoning skills through reading, discussion, presentation, and writing.
Demonstrate increased confidence in speaking skills.
Develop the ability to learn from others' presentations.
Prepare the right tools for presentations.
Demonstrate teamwork and group work skills that contribute to the group they participate in.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: U1 SI Units - U1 SI Systems of Units
Hafta 3: U1 Types of Measurement - U2 Numbers and Basic Math
Hafta 4: U2 Large Numbers - U2 Analyzing Quantities
Hafta 5: U2 Working with Numbers - U2 Mathematics
Hafta 9: U3 Physics - U4 The Periodical Table
Hafta 10: U4 Chemical Reactions - U4 Chemistry
Hafta 11: U5 Tables and Graphs - U5 The Scientific Method
Hafta 13: U5 Research Activities - U6 Shapes

English for Engineering Course Booklet

courses.name: Engineering Mathematics I
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain the fundamentals of mathematics from an engineering perspective and to discuss and apply the techniques needed to solve real-life problems.
courses.description: Functions, limits and continuity, derivatives, applications of derivatives, integration, applications of definite integrals, improper integrals.
learning_outcomes: Understand the definition of a function, know the properties of transcendental functions, and be able to graph them. Understand and apply the principles of shifting and scaling in functions.
Know the definition and properties of the limit of a function. Know the definition and properties of continuity of a function. Explain and use the fundamental theorems of continuity.

Know the definitions of tangent and normal lines for the graph of a function. Understand the definition and properties of the derivative. Calculate derivatives of transcendental functions. Apply the chain rule. Calculate derivatives of inverse functions. Know the rules for differentiating composite functions. Understand linearization of a function. Know the applications of derivatives. Calculate limits using L'Hôpital's rule.
Know the definitions of definite and indefinite integrals. Explain and apply integration techniques. Explain and apply the fundamental theorems of calculus.
Understand the applications of integrals in finding area and volume. Understand and explain the concept of improper integrals and the convergence of improper integrals.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions: functions and graphs, composite function, shifting and scaling graphs
Hafta 2: Trigonometric functions, exponential functions, inverse functions, logarithmic functions, inverse trigonometric functions
Hafta 3: Limit and Continuity: definition of limit, limit of a function and limit laws, one-sided limits
Hafta 4: Limits at infinity and infinite limits, continuity
Hafta 5: Derivative: derivative of a function, equations of tangent and normal lines, differentiation rules, derivative at a point, derivatives of trigonometric functions, chain rule
Hafta 6: Derivative of implicit functions, derivatives of inverse functions, derivatives of exponential and logarithmic functions, linear approximation
Hafta 7: Applications of Derivatives: maximum/minimum (extreme) values of functions, mean value theorem, first and second derivative tests, increasing and decreasing functions, concavity
Hafta 8: Asymptotes of graphs, graph sketching, indeterminate forms and L'Hopital's rule, applied optimization
Hafta 9: Integral: antiderivatives, area and approximation with finite sums, sigma notation and limits of finite sums, definite integral
Hafta 10: Fundamental theorem of calculus, indefinite integrals, substitution method
Hafta 11: Integration Methods: trigonometric integrals, integration by parts
Hafta 12: Integration of rational functions (partial fraction decomposition), trigonometric substitution method
Hafta 13: Applications of Integrals: areas between curves
Hafta 14: Volume by slicing, volume by cylindrical shells xxx
resources: Thomas' Calculus: Global Edition, 12/e by George B. Thomas, Jr., Maurice D. Weir, and Joel Hass, ISBN -10: 0321643631, ISBN-13: 9780321643636, Published by Pearson Higher Education ©2010, 1236 pp. packaged with MyMathLab MyMathLab stand-alone.
Calculus: Early Transcendentals (Stewart's Calculus Series) by James Stewart

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the mechanics-related concepts required in classical physics at a good level and enable them to use these concepts in specialized courses in their departments.
courses.description: Measurement and unit systems, vectors, kinematics in one dimension, kinematics in two and three dimensions, Newton's laws of motion, Newton's law of universal gravitation, work and energy, conservation of energy, linear momentum, rotational motion, angular momentum, and general rotational motion.
learning_outcomes: Analyzes static, kinematic, and dynamic processes.
Produces solutions to structures and problems related to static, kinematic, and dynamic processes.
Applies learned concepts to special physics subfields.
Models static, kinematic, and dynamic processes and solves these models.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Measurements and Estimation Methods
Hafta 3: Kinematics in Two and Three Dimensions, Vectors
Hafta 4: Dynamics: Newton's Laws of Motion – I
Hafta 5: Dynamics: Newton's Laws of Motion – II
Hafta 6: Friction, Circular Motion, Drag Forces
Hafta 7: Gravitation and Newton's Synthesis – I
Hafta 8: Gravitation and Newton's Synthesis – II
resources: Lecture Slides
Physics for Scientists & Engineers with Modern Physics, 4th Ed., by D. C. Giancoli, Pearson Education Inc., 2009
Course Notes

courses.name: Türk Dili I
courses.purpose: Dilin fert ve millet hayatındaki önemini ve Türk dilinin yapısal özelliklerini kavratmak, Türkçenin tarihi dönemlerini öğretmek, Türkçenin ses ve şekil yapısını kavratıp öğrencinin dinlediklerini, okuduklarını, gördüklerini ve düşündüklerini hem söz hem de yazılı olarak planlı ve akıcı bir şekilde ifade etme yeteneklerini geliştirmek.
courses.description: Dilin tanımı, doğuşu ve özellikleri, dilin türleri, yeryüzündeki diller ve türleri, Türk dilinin dünya dilleri arasındaki yeri, Türk dilinin temel özellikleri, ses bilgisi, tümce bilgisi ve biçim bilgisi, Türkçenin söz varlığı, Türkçenin güncel sorunları.
learning_outcomes: Türkçeyi doğru, güzel ve başarılı bir üslupla konuşur, dilin parçalarını birleştirir.
Dilin tanımını ve işlevlerini ifade eder.
Türk dilini tarihsel gelişimini açıklar.
Okuduğu, dinlediği bir metni veya izlediği bir programı doğru yorumlar.
Çevresindeki dil kirliliğine yol açan kelimelerin dil üzerindeki etkilerini hesaplar, kültürün dil üzerindeki etkisini ayırt eder.
Sözlük ve yazım kılavuzu kullanır.
Çevresinde sıklıkla kullanılan yabancı kelimelerin Türkçe karşılıklarını önerir.
Dünya dilleri içerisinde Türk dilinin yerini ayırt eder ve Türkçeyi bilim dili olarak kullanır.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Dilin Tanımı ve Özellikleri, Dil ve Kültür, Dil ve Toplum, Dil ve İletişim, Dil ve Düşünce. Türkçenin Dünya Dilleri Arasındaki Yeri. Dillerin Sınıflandırılması: kaynak bakımından dünya dilleri, yapı bakımından dünya dilleri. Dil Farklılaşması.

Hafta 2: Türk Dilinin Gelişimi ve Tarihsel Dönemleri, Türkçenin Yaşı. Türk Yazı Dillerinin ve Lehçelerinin Tasnifi. Dillerin Doğuşu İle İlgili Kuramlar. Dilin Türleri: lehçe, şive, ağız, yazı dili, konuşma dili, ölçünlü dil, uygarlık dili, argo, ölü dil, yapay dil…

Hafta 3: Türkçenin Yazımında Kullanılan Alfabeler: Göktürk alfabesi, Mani alfabesi, Uygur alfabesi… Türk Dili Çalışmaları, Yazı ve Dil Devrimi, Atatürk ve Türk Dili. Türkiye Türkçesinin Fonetik Özellikleri, Türkçenin Hece Yapısı.
Hafta 4: Ses Uyumları: büyük ünlü uyumu, küçük ünlü uyumu. Ses Olayları: ses benzeşmesi, ses değişmesi, ses türemesi, ses düşmesi, ses daralması, göçüşme. Metin üzerinde uygulama
Hafta 5: Parçalar Üstü Ses Birimleri: vurgu, tonlama, durak. Biçim Bilgisiyle İlgili Temel Kavramlar, Türkçenin Ekleri: yapım ekleri, çekim ekleri. Metin üzerinde uygulama
Hafta 6: Türkçede Kökler, Sözcük Yapımı: örnekseme, türetme, karma, kısaltma… Sözcük Türleri: anlam bakımından sözcükler, tür ve görev bakımından sözcükler.
Hafta 8: Sözcük Öbekleri: tamlamalar, ünlem grubu, unvan grubu, sayı öbeği, ikilemeler, birleşik eylem öbeği, isim-fiil/sıfat-fiil/zarf-fiil öbeği, kısaltma öbekleri.
Hafta 9: Cümlenin Ögeleri: yüklem, özne, nesne, yer tamlayıcısı, belirteç tümleci, cümle dışı ögeler. Metin üzerinde uygulama.
Hafta 10: Cümle Türleri: yapısına göre tümceler, yüklemin türüne göre tümceler, yüklemin yerine göre tümceler, anlamlarına göre tümceler.
Hafta 11: Türkçenim Söz Varlığı, Türkçenin Anlatım Gücü. Deyimler, Atasözleri, Deyimler ve Atasözleri Arasındaki Farklar, Alıntı Sözler, Ağız Ögeleri.

Hafta 12: Diller Arası Etkileşim ve Türkçe, Dillerin Zenginlik Alanları, Tarihsel Derinlik ve Coğrafi Yaygınlık, Söz Varlığı ve Anlatım Gücü, Atasözleri ve Anlatım Gücü, Akrabalık Adlarında Çeşitlilik, İlişki ve Nezaket Sözleri.

Hafta 13: Dünya Dillerinin Türk Diline Etkisi, Eski Türkçe Döneminde Yabancı Dillerin Etkisi, Batı Dillerinin Türkçeye Etkisi. Kitle İletişim Araçlarında Türkçenin Kullanımı, Sosyal Medyada Türkçenin Kullanımı.
Hafta 14: Söyleyiş Bozuklukları, Konuşma ve Yazıda Kısır Söz Varlığı, Yabancı Sözlerin Yoğunluğu, Yabancı Dille Öğretim, Bilim Terimleri, İş Yaşamında Türkçe.

courses.name: Atatürk Principles and History of Revolution II
courses.purpose: To help students understand the principles underlying Atatürk's reforms and revolutions and enable them to interpret and analyze them. It also aims to teach university students the recent history of Turkey.
courses.description: The course examines the founding history of the Republic of Turkey in the light of Mustafa Kemal Atatürk's principles and reforms, covering basic definitions, concepts, and events within the framework of the 16th and 20th centuries.
learning_outcomes: To establish connections between our country's past, present, and history.
To understand the relationships among historical events.
To gain knowledge about current issues through the acquired knowledge base.
To form an opinion about the historical heritage of the Republic of Turkey.
To acquire the ability to synthesize national and universal values.
To use the awareness gained in the course in academic and/or personal life.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Reorganization period: politics and society between 1920 and 1938

Hafta 2: Turkish political climate between 1920 and 1938: political parties in the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, government-opposition relations, and the establishment of the multi-party system in Turkey
Hafta 3: Economic thought and practices in the new Turkey: the nation-state and the reorganization of the economic structure
Hafta 4: Turkish foreign policy in the interwar context
Hafta 7: The nation-state and national culture relations: studies in language, history, and culture
Hafta 9: Reorganization after the Second World War
Hafta 10: Turkey in the 1950s and the Democratic Party: domestic policy practices and foreign policy of the Democratic Party
Hafta 11: Economic and social policy in Turkey between 1960 and 1980
Hafta 12: Social changes and struggles in Turkey between 1960 and 1980
Hafta 13: The world and Turkey during the Cold War period
Hafta 14: The world and Turkey after the Cold War period

courses.name: Computer Programming II
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamentals of the Java language and to develop above-intermediate problem-solving skills by using various Java language constructs such as branching, loops, data types, arrays, classes, objects, inheritance, and polymorphism.
courses.description: Java fundamentals and basic data types, input/output, branching statements, loops, classes, methods, objects, arrays, inheritance, polymorphism, and interfaces.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic building blocks of Java
Use different language constructs in problem solving
Understand and apply object-oriented concepts such as classes, objects, inheritance, and polymorphism
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Java Fundamentals and Basic Data Types
Hafta 2: Java Fundamentals and Basic Data Types
Hafta 7: Defining Classes and Methods (Midterm Exam)
Hafta 8: More About Objects and Methods
Hafta 9: More About Objects and Methods
resources: Java, An Introduction to Problem Solving & Programming, 8th Global Edition, Walter Savitch, Pearson Publishing

courses.name: Engineering English II
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the discursive and cognitive features of academic article, project, and research writing.
courses.description: The course covers academic writing processes, practice in writing academic texts on a specific topic, introduction of the basic factors involved in academic writing and the organization of academic writing, differences between academic writing style and personal writing styles, grammar in academic writing, and strategies needed to produce increasingly complex texts and create a complete text. The course focuses on bibliography writing and research report and article writing.
learning_outcomes: Develop problem-solving and idea-generation skills and transform these ideas into written texts that can be effectively communicated in academic appropriateness.
Recognize academic discourse and academic vocabulary together with academic writing processes.
Examine ways of organizing data.
Plan how analogies and comparisons lead to evaluations.
Examine the importance of clarity, honesty, reality, and coherence in developing texts.

Hafta 10: Using evidence to support an idea, examining opposing ideas

courses.name: Engineering Mathematics II
courses.purpose: Students will be able to analyze using various advanced computational methods.
courses.description: Sequences and series, polar coordinates, vectors, limits and continuity in multivariable functions; partial derivatives and their applications, double and triple integrals.
learning_outcomes: Understands and explains the concepts of infinite sequences and series and investigates their convergence.
Understands and explains infinite series and their convergence using various convergence tests such as the integral test, comparison test, ratio and root tests, and the alternating series test.

Understands and explains the convergence behavior of infinite series, including power series, and finds intervals and radii of convergence by using the concepts of absolute and conditional convergence. Understands and explains Taylor and Maclaurin series expansions and the approximation of functions and their integrals and derivatives using Taylor and Maclaurin series expansions.

Understands and explains multivariable analysis concepts including vectors, lines, planes in space, polar coordinates, multivariable functions, limits, continuity, partial derivatives, directional derivatives, extrema of multivariable functions, and the Lagrange method.

Understands and explains multiple integrals, including double and triple integrals in different coordinate systems (rectangular, cylindrical, and spherical) and substitutions used to simplify integrals over complex regions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Chapter 10.2-10.4: Infinite Series, Integral test, Comparison test
Hafta 3: Chapter 10.5-10.6: Ratio and root tests, Alternating series, Absolute and conditional convergence
Hafta 4: Chapter 10.7: Power series (radius of convergence and interval of convergence)
Hafta 5: Chapter 10.8-10:10: Taylor and Maclaurin series
Hafta 6: Chapter 11. Parametrization of curves in the plane, Calculus with parametric curves, Polar coordinates
Hafta 7: Chapter 12. Vectors, Lines and planes in space
Hafta 8: Chapter 14.1-14.2: Concept of multivariable functions (level curve and level surface), Limits and continuity in higher dimensions
Hafta 9: Chapter 14.3-14.6: Partial derivatives, Chain rule, Directional derivative and gradient vector, Tangent plane
Hafta 10: Chapter 14.7-14.8: Local extrema and saddle points in two-variable functions, Lagrange multipliers method
Hafta 11: Chapter 15-1:15.4: Multiple Integrals, Double integral over a rectangle, Double integral over general regions, Area with double integrals
Hafta 12: Chapter 15.5-15.7: Triple integrals in rectangular coordinates, Triple integrals in cylindrical and spherical coordinates
Hafta 13: Chapter 15.5-15.7: Triple integrals in rectangular coordinates, Triple integrals in cylindrical and spherical coordinates
Hafta 14: Chapter 15.8: Change of variables in multiple integrals

courses.name: Physics II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental background in electricity and magnetism required for physics education.
courses.description: Coulomb force, electric field, electric flux, Gauss's law, electric potential, capacitors, current and resistance, direct current circuits, Kirchhoff's laws, magnetic field, Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, induction, Faraday's law, Lenz's law, inductance, energy in magnetic fields, oscillations in LC circuits, and electromagnetic waves.

Analyzes the force and electric field created by charged systems.
Knows the technological uses of capacitors and designs capacitor circuits.
Analyzes electric current and conduction.
Understands the formation of magnetic fields and magnetic force.
Applies electromagnetic induction, Faraday's law, and Lenz's law in electric circuits.

courses.name: Introduction to Software Engineering
courses.purpose: To teach the fundamentals, basic concepts, and terminology of the software engineering discipline. It also aims to develop abstraction, problem-solving, and a systemic perspective.
courses.description: This course covers the basic components of software such as fundamental computer concepts, operating systems, and compilers, as well as software types. It also examines the basic concepts of software engineering, software process models, requirements engineering, system and architectural design using basic UML, software testing and maintenance processes, and the basic principles of software project management, quality management, and configuration management.

Explain the fundamentals of software project management and key topics such as cost, scheduling, quality, and risk.
Acquire the knowledge required for the analysis, design, testing, and maintenance of software systems.
Explain software product and process quality.
Gain knowledge about the use of methods, techniques, and tools related to software engineering.

Students understand the responsibilities of an engineer in terms of occupational safety.

courses.name: Turkish Language II
courses.purpose: To help students understand the fundamental principles of Turkish, interpret and analyze texts, use Turkish correctly and effectively according to diction rules, develop their native language skills by fostering appreciation for Turkish in all its aspects, and improve their reading, listening, and writing abilities.
courses.description: Composition information and principles of written composition; punctuation marks; spelling rules; thought writings such as articles, essays, columns, criticism, and interviews; narrative writing types; artistic writings such as poetry, story, novel, and theater; scientific writings; correspondence types such as petitions, minutes, decisions, reports, resumes, and letters; reading and reading habits; reading types and reading comprehension strategies; critical reading; listening and factors affecting the listening process; relationship between reading and listening; oral expression, basic concepts related to speaking, correct, beautiful and effective speaking, body language and its place in oral expression, and types of speaking such as prepared and unprepared speech.
learning_outcomes: Expresses feelings and thoughts effectively in written and oral form.
Learns how to listen and read effectively.
Can use Turkish in thesis writing rules in academic theses (bachelor's, master's, doctorate).
Gains critical thinking skills and evaluates acquired knowledge and skills with a critical approach.
Gains the ability to analyze, interpret, and evaluate field-related information.
Recognizes types of writing and composition.
Can examine literary and thought works and apply rhetorical practices on these works.
Learns the basic structure of spoken language and gains the ability to speak effectively and beautifully.

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms

Model complex problems mathematically using basic data types
Use tree structures to solve problems
Design and develop algorithms to solve simple problems
Apply efficient operations on basic Abstract Data Types (ADT) and sets

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.purpose: This course is designed to improve students' knowledge of the most common structures of discrete mathematics, such as mathematical logic (for analyzing propositions and proving theorems), algorithms (for analyzing their complexity), counting, relations and their representations, graphs and their interpretation.
courses.description: Logic. Mathematical proof methods. Algorithms. Divisibility. Modular arithmetic. Induction and recursion. Basic principles of counting. Permutation. Combination. Binomial. Inclusion-exclusion principle. Pigeonhole principle. Relations. Graphs and Trees.
learning_outcomes: Construct logical arguments.
Use basic mathematical notation and concepts in practice.
Learn and practice the basic concepts of mathematical proofs (direct proof, proof by contradiction, mathematical induction).
Explain standard logical symbols.
Solve major combinatorics and counting problems.
Simplify complex mathematical expressions and apply general formulas in specific contexts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Foundations: Logic and Proofs: Propositional Logic, Applications of Propositional Logic, Propositional Equivalences

Hafta 4: Basic Structures: Sets, Functions, Sequences, Sums, Cardinality of Sets

Hafta 10: Counting: The Basics of Counting, Pigeonhole Principle, Permutations and Combinations, Binomial Coefficients and Identities, Generalized Permutations and Combinations
Hafta 11: Advanced Counting Techniques: Applications of Recurrence Relations, Solutions of Linear Recurrence Relations, Generating Functions, Inclusion-Exclusion

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain some fundamental concepts of linear algebra and provide a perspective for using them especially in engineering problems.

learning_outcomes: Solve linear equation systems.
Determine matrix properties and find the inverse of a matrix.
Calculate determinants.
Explain vector spaces and perform operations on them.
Explain the relationships among linear independence, span, and bases.
Determine eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and diagonalize matrices.

courses.name: Object-Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with knowledge of concepts such as inheritance, polymorphism, and interfaces in order to develop effective and flexible object-oriented software, to enable them to perform unit testing of object-oriented software, and to gain the ability to use design patterns in object-oriented solutions.
courses.description: Fundamentals of object-oriented software development. Object-oriented modeling using UML (Unified Modeling Language). Overview of the Java language. Inheritance, polymorphism, and interfaces. Transition from basic structures to maintainable software. Unit testing. Design patterns. Concurrent programming. An integrated sample application.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic structures required to express problem solutions in an object-oriented way.
Express object-oriented solutions with a standard notation.
Produce flexible object-oriented solutions by effectively using inheritance, polymorphism, and interfaces.
Select the most effective object-oriented solution for a problem.
Perform unit tests of developed object-oriented solutions.
Understand and apply the basic concepts of design patterns.
Select the most appropriate design patterns to express an object-oriented solution.
Use what they have learned in solving current, practical problems.
Prepare technical reports from object-oriented problem solving to testing.

Hafta 2: Object-Oriented Modeling Using UML: Basic principles, concepts, and structures of object-oriented modeling. Most commonly used notations in UML: use case, class, object, and sequence diagrams. A case study on object-oriented modeling.
Hafta 6: System Design: Parts of the system.
Hafta 7: System Design: Determining design goals.
Hafta 9: Object Design: Reuse of solution patterns.
Hafta 11: Object Design: OCL (Object Constraint Language).

courses.name: Human Computer Interaction

Understands models related to communication and interaction processes and interprets design criteria in educational software.
Knows and uses interface design principles, interprets and analyzes the task factor in interface design, and completes needs and task analysis.
Knows and applies usability test criteria, uses usability engineering principles, and applies cognitive-computer interaction in educational software.
Analyzes HCI principles in web-based examples and in computer software.
Synthesizes communication and information technologies on the axis of Human-Computer Interaction.

Know the relationships between variables and storage

courses.name: Internship I

learning_outcomes: Will be able to evaluate the practical reflections of the theoretical knowledge gained in university education.

courses.name: Data Ethics

Understands the importance of ethics in research.
Learns and applies experimental research, design, and reporting techniques.

courses.purpose: Modern ve yaygın kullanılan Python programlama dilinin bilimsel çalışmalar için sağladığı olanakların öğrenilmesi.

Bilgisayar ile problem çözmede farklı paradigmaların tanıtılması ve karşılaştırılması
Sayısal ve analitik modellerin karşılaştırılması becerisinin kazanılması

courses.name: Current Topics in Aviation Technologies
courses.purpose: To enable students to follow topics related to aviation and space technologies in written and visual scientific publications, and to develop critical reading and presentation skills.
courses.description: Introduction to Nature Journal, The Economist, Scientific American and Science Magazine, including editorials, news, research articles, podcasts, briefing sections, special reports, Babbage podcast and blog archives, as well as scientific American and Science Magazine online archives. The course also covers readings on aviation and space technologies, academic and engineering vocabulary, and project-based activities.
learning_outcomes: Critically read articles on aviation and space topics published in international scientific journals.
Listen to and understand podcasts published in international scientific media.
Discuss current aviation and space topics as presented in international media, evaluate them critically, and deliver presentations on them.
Develop academic and engineering vocabulary.

courses.name: Electro-Optical Systems for Aerospace Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the design of electro-optical systems from a systems engineering perspective.
courses.description: Overview of electro-optical systems, image chains for electro-optical systems, atmospheric radiance calculations, the electromagnetic spectrum of light, optical layout of telescope systems, telescope types and an introduction to ray tracing optical design software, imaging sensors: time delay integration (TDI) charge-coupled devices (CCD) and complementary metal oxide semiconductor (CMOS) devices, sensor noise analysis, optical system performance evaluation metrics: modulation transfer function, signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) calculations, general image quality equation (GIQE) and image rating, image quality degradation factors: jitter and blur.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes the fundamentals of electro-optical systems.
Recognizes, explains, and applies image formation and the image chain.
Recognizes, evaluates, and relates the systems engineering perspective of electro-optical systems.
Recognizes and uses the electromagnetic spectrum of light.
Uses ray tracing software for optical design.

Hafta 3: Atmospheric radiance calculation, electromagnetic spectrum of light, and simulation tools
Hafta 4: Telescope systems, optical layout, and telescope types
Hafta 5: Introduction to ray tracing software for optical design - 1
Hafta 6: Introduction to ray tracing software for optical design - 2
Hafta 7: Introduction to ray tracing software for optical design - 3

Hafta 13: Image quality degradation factors caused by satellites: jitter and blur
Hafta 14: Jitter and blur calculation for a case study

courses.name: Remote Sensing
courses.purpose: To provide knowledge about remote sensing, image processing, and the analysis of geospatial information.
courses.description: Remote sensing satellites, sensors used for remote sensing, and an introduction to radar and lidar systems. Digital image processing and analysis, point operations, algebraic operations, filtering, geographic information systems, information models, and structures.
learning_outcomes: Understand energy sources and radiation principles
Understand energy movements in the atmosphere
Understand energy interactions on the Earth's surface
Understand digital image concepts and data acquisition
Understand the characteristics of remote sensing systems
Apply remote sensing successfully
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Multispectral, hyperspectral, and thermal sensors
resources: Introduction to Remote Sensing, Fifth Edition, James B. Campbell, Randolph H. Wynne
Image processing and GIS for remote sensing, Techniques and applications, 2nd Edition, Jian Guo Liu and Philippa J. Mason, Wiley Blackwell

courses.name: Havacılık Kontrol Sistemleri
courses.purpose: Modern control theory adopts a state-space model that uses a set of first-order differential equations to describe system input-output dynamics.
courses.description: Review of linear algebra, eigenvalues and eigenvectors; state-space representations of systems with transfer functions; solution of time-invariant state equations; controllability and observability; stability of systems; state-space design of control systems; state variable feedback; state observers; introduction to optimal control; linear quadratic regulator.
learning_outcomes: State-space representation, transfer function systems, canonical forms of state-space equations
Transformation of system models from transfer function to state-space models and vice versa
Solution of time-invariant state equations, controllability and observability, stability
Direct placement approach in control system design
State observer
Quadratic optimal regulator systems
Control design with Matlab software
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of linear algebra, eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Hafta 2: State-space representations of transfer function systems
Hafta 8: State-space design of control systems
Hafta 12: Introduction to optimal control, linear quadratic regulator
Hafta 13: Control design with Matlab software
Hafta 14: Control design with Matlab software
resources: Dorf, Richard C., and Bishop, Robert H. Modern control systems. 12th Edition, Pearson, 2011.
Ogata, Katsuhiko. Modern Control Engineering. 5th Edition, Pearson, 2010.

courses.name: Introduction to Astronomy
courses.purpose: This course is a one-semester introduction to astronomy. It aims to provide students with fundamental knowledge of astronomy in the light of the laws of physics and to help them understand their place in the universe in both cosmic and evolutionary terms. The course also explains not only what we know about the universe and celestial objects, but also how we obtain this knowledge.
courses.description: Astronomical length and distance units, constellations, the celestial sphere, motions of celestial objects, time calculations, electromagnetic wave types and related calculations, telescopes and detectors, members of the Solar System, galaxies, modeling of the universe, dark matter and dark energy, the search for extraterrestrial life, and space vehicles designed for astronomical purposes.
learning_outcomes: Describe how some constellations are found in the sky and define the celestial sphere.

Explain the reference planes of coordinate systems used in astronomy, how angles are measured, describe the motions of celestial objects in the light of these systems, and perform simple time calculations.
Explain the structure of telescopes used in astronomy and remote sensing, list their types, and identify some basic devices used with these telescopes.

Explain in simple terms what the basic parameters of stars such as brightness and distance mean and how they are determined, and briefly describe how stars evolve, end their lives, and what they become after death.
List some space vehicles used for astronomical purposes together with their basic functions and explain their contributions to science and engineering at a basic level.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ne kadar küçüğüz?: Astronomide birimler ve uzaklıklar
Hafta 2: Gökyüzünü tanıyalım: Takımyıldızlar ve Gökküresi
Hafta 3: Koordinat Sistemleri, Gök Cisimlerinin Hareketleri ve Zaman Hesabı
Hafta 5: Teleskoplar ve diğer gözlem araçları
Hafta 6: Güneş ve güneş sistemi üyeleri
Hafta 11: Yıldızların ölümü: Süpernovalar, Beyaz Cüceler, Nötron Yıldızları ve Karadelikler
Hafta 12: Samanyolu galaksisi, diğer galaksiler ve karanlık madde
Hafta 13: Evren, evrenin modellenmesi ve karanlık madde
Hafta 15: Astronomi hedefli uzay araçları ve uzaydaki gözlemevleri
resources: Bradley W. Carroll, Dale A. Ostlie, An Introduction to Modern Astrophysics, Cambridge University Press, 2nd ed., 2017, ISBN-13: 978-1108422161
Roger A. Freedman, Robert Geller, William J. Kaufmann III, Universe: Stars & Galaxies, W.H. Freeman & Company, New York, 11th ed., 2019, ISBN-13: 978-1-319-22797-5

courses.name: Algorithms for Engineering
courses.purpose: This course teaches fundamental algorithms and algorithm design techniques that can be used to design solutions for real-life problems.
courses.description: Introduction, mathematical foundations, asymptotic notations, iterative and recursive sorting algorithms, heap and heap sort, counting sort and radix sort, binary search trees, AVL and RB trees, B-trees, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, amortized cost analysis, graphs: BFS, DFS, topological sorting, minimum spanning tree algorithms, shortest path algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Analyze spatial and temporal complexity of algorithms
Design algorithms for new problems using algorithm design techniques
Select the most appropriate algorithmic technique for the problem to be solved
Understand the foundations of advanced data structures
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, mathematical foundations, asymptotic notations
Hafta 2: Iterative sorting algorithms: bubble sort, selection sort, insertion sort
Hafta 3: Recursive algorithms: merge sort, quick sort, finding the median
Hafta 5: Counting sort and radix sort
Hafta 6: Binary search trees, AVL and RB trees
resources: Introduction to Algorithms, 3/e, Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest and Stein, MIT Press

courses.name: Robot Vision
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to introduce the basic principles of robotic vision and image processing used in robotic systems. Students will learn how robots use cameras to detect objects, estimate depth, and understand their environment. The course focuses on simple vision methods and their applications in robot perception and localization.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of robotic vision. Topics include image processing basics, feature detection and matching, object recognition and tracking, camera models, camera calibration, stereo vision, depth estimation, motion estimation, and vision-based robot localization. Simple applications of vision in robotic systems are also introduced.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts of robotic vision and image processing.
Define basic methods for feature detection, feature matching, and object tracking.
Understand basic camera models and camera calibration principles.
Explain the basic concepts of stereo vision and depth estimation.
Understand how vision can be used for robot motion estimation and localization.
resources: Hartley, Richard, and Andrew Zisserman. Multiple view geometry in computer vision. Cambridge university press, 2003.
Corke, P. Robotics, Vision and Control: Fundamental Algorithms in MATLAB. Springer, 2011.
Roland Siegwart, Illah Reza Nourbakhsh and Davide Scaramuzza, Introduction to Autonomous Mobile Robots, second edition

courses.name: Cryptography
courses.purpose: This course presents encryption systems, algorithms, and their theory.
courses.description: Mathematical preparation, symmetric encryption, block encryption, DES, AES, public-key encryption, RSA, message authentication and hash functions, digital signature and authentication.
learning_outcomes: Learn the theory of basic cryptography and encryption/decryption algorithms.
Learn to implement encryption algorithms.
Can build cryptosystems using encryption applications.
Can apply encryption algorithms to create information and network security mechanisms.
Can use authentication and message verification techniques to develop identity management.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Approaches to security: OSI architectural model, attacks, systems, mechanisms and models
Hafta 2: Symmetric algorithms: classical encryption techniques
Hafta 4: Finite fields: modular arithmetic, Euclid algorithm, polynomial arithmetic
Hafta 6: Ensuring information confidentiality with symmetric encryption
Hafta 10: Key exchange and other cryptosystems
Hafta 11: Message authentication and hash functions
Hafta 13: Digital signature and authentication protocols

courses.name: Statistical Computing
courses.purpose: The aim of Statistical Computing is to learn how to use the Matlab tool to analyze data in tables or surveys, interpret the obtained results, and use graphs to understand and examine the data.
courses.description: Data and scale type, descriptive statistics, discrete and continuous distributions, correlation analysis, T-tests, variance analysis, clustering analysis, plotting and visualization.
learning_outcomes: Distinguish different types of data and variables. Survey analysis.
Use Matlab to perform statistical analysis.
Determine relationships between data.
Interpret results obtained by statistical methods.
Visualize the results of statistical methods by plotting.

courses.name: Web Design and Development
courses.purpose: The course explains the methods and tools used for designing and developing web pages with interactive content.

PHP with MySQL. PHP with XML. JavaScript and DOM. Creating HTML forms with Ajax. Ajax with databases. Ajax with XML.

Understand the use of event-driven programming in JavaScript.
Use AJAX tools to create web pages that connect to servers.
Use PHP for server-side programming and understand advanced PHP syntax.
Perform relational database operations using embedded SQL commands within PHP code.

courses.name: Introduction to Information Systems
courses.purpose: The course aims to provide students with a solid foundation for the commercial use of information technologies in a rapidly changing environment and to encourage discussion of critical issues surrounding the use of IT in organizations. It also aims to increase awareness of ethical issues related to technology use in business contexts and to enable students to use information technology as a tool for performing basic business tasks.
courses.description: Information systems architectures, database management systems, e-commerce, telecommunications, software and hardware standards, Internet/web-based systems, data storage, data mining, agent-based systems, and the social impacts of information systems.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic concepts and terminology of information systems and related technologies.
Evaluate information systems, assess user needs, develop solution proposals, and evaluate implementation proposals.
Communicate effectively about information technologies with salespeople, technical experts, users, colleagues, and managers.
Understand the value IT provides by improving the management process through better access to information.
Appreciate the challenges encountered in developing and using IT-based business solutions.
Understand the system development life cycle and software development methods.

courses.name: Distributed Systems and Algorithms
courses.purpose: To understand distributed systems and their design, to grasp the general philosophy of their implementation from both system and hardware perspectives, and to follow and understand current trends in the field.
courses.description: Distributed system architecture, hardware and software, parallel systems, communication in distributed systems, performance and scalability, client/server models, RPC, synchronization problems in distributed systems, transactions and processors, distributed file systems, and distributed shared memory.
learning_outcomes: Have basic knowledge in the field of distributed systems.
Learn the basic principles of parallel systems.
Be able to perform performance modeling and analysis in distributed systems.
Be able to develop software for parallel and distributed systems.

Hafta 3: Basic distributed system structure: Software
Hafta 4: Basic distributed system structure: Parallel systems
Hafta 9: Synchronization problems in distributed systems
Hafta 10: Transactions and processors in parallel systems

courses.name: Parallel and Distributed Computing
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamentals of parallel program design and parallel computer architectures.
courses.description: This course covers parallel architectures. It also teaches parallel application development using OpenMP for shared-memory parallel architectures and MPI for distributed-memory systems.
learning_outcomes: Classify and compare parallel computer architectures.
Develop message-passing programs.
Understand speedup, efficiency, and scalability in parallel algorithms.
Analyze the complexities of parallel programs.
Understand basic parallel programming techniques.
Transform sequential algorithms into parallel algorithms using parallel programming techniques.
Design parallel algorithms.
Write parallel programs that can run in a multi-computer environment.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Classification of Parallel Computers Parallel Architectures
Hafta 2: Communication Methods Multi-Computer Platforms Speedup, Efficiency and Scalability
resources: B. Wilkinson and M. Allen, Parallel Programming, Prentice-Hall, 2005

courses.name: Bulut Bilişim Temelleri
courses.purpose: Bu ders; hizmet modelleri, dağıtım modelleri, mimari bileşenler ve modern trendler dahil olmak üzere bulut bilişimin temel kavramlarını tanıtır. Öğrencilere bulut tabanlı sistemleri tasarlayabilmeleri ve bu sistemlerle çalışabilmeleri için hem teorik bilgi hem de pratik uygulama becerisi kazandırmayı amaçlar.
courses.description: Hizmet Modelleri; Dağıtım Modelleri; Sanallaştırma; Soyutlama; Depolama Servisleri (Blok, Dosya, Nesne); Bilgi İşlem Servisleri (Sanal Makineler, Konteynerler, Sunucusuz); Ağ Servisleri; Bulutta Veri Tabanları ve Analitik; Bulut Güvenliği ve Kimlik ve Erişim Yönetimi (IAM); Modern Trendler; Otomatik Ölçeklendirme ve Bulut İzleme;

IaaS, PaaS ve SaaS hizmet modelleri arasındaki farkları açıklayabilecektir.
Genel (Public), Özel (Private) ve Hibrit (Hybrid) bulut dağıtım modellerini karşılaştırabilecektir.
Sanallaştırma, konteynerler, depolama ve ağ hizmetlerini mimari bileşenler olarak anlayabilecektir.
Ölçeklenebilirlik, sunucusuz mimari (serverless), çoklu bulut (multi-cloud) ve DevOps gibi modern bulut eğilimlerini değerlendirebilecektir.
Bulut güvenliği, kimlik yönetimi ve izleme (monitoring) kavramlarının önemini kavrayabilecektir.

courses.name: Cyber Security

Karmaşık sorunları çözmek için programlama teknikleri tasarlar, geliştirir ve uygular.
İşbirliğine dayalı takım çalışması ve etkili iletişim dahil olmak üzere profesyonel davranış sergiler; ağları ve ağ hizmetlerini yönetir.
Bilgisayar sistemlerinin kuruluşlar, toplum ve bireyler üzerindeki etik, yasal ve düzenleyici etkilerini analiz edebilir.
Riskleri belirlemek, tehditleri değerlendirmek ve bilgisayar varlıklarını ve verilerini korumak için çözümler geliştirebilir.
Komut dosyası yazımını ve komut dosyası dillerinin rolü, sözdizimi ve anlambilimi hakkında bildiklerini ve anladıklarını açıklayabilir.

Hafta 9: Sosyal Mühendislik ve Oturum Ele Geçirme

Hafta 12: Kablosuz, IoT ve OT Güvenliği
Hafta 13: Güvenlik Operasyonları, AI/ML'nin Rolü, Güvenlik Yönetişimi

Güvenlik kurallarını uygular.
Bir güvenlik duvarını yönetip izleyebilecektir.
Güvenli bir ağ tasarlayabilecektir.

learning_outcomes: Have knowledge about wireless systems.
Have knowledge about mobile computing development environments.
Have knowledge about current technologies in mobile computing.
Learn the basic principles of mobile computing.

courses.name: Defense Systems Software
courses.purpose: This course is designed to teach students the fundamentals and applications of defense systems software. Its main goal is to develop students' ability to understand, design, implement, and evaluate software requirements for defense systems. It also aims to improve their ability to solve complex problems using software engineering principles in defense systems and to introduce current defense technologies, industry standards, and career opportunities in the field.
courses.description: This course explains the characteristics of defense systems software such as real-time operation, safety-criticality, mission-criticality, embeddedness, interaction with the external environment, reactivity, and high quality. It also addresses the challenges of developing defense software and examines the differences between defense software and commercial software.
learning_outcomes: Basic knowledge and practical skills in defense systems software.
Ability to take part in the design of complex systems.
Ability to design military communication and data security software.
Ability to manage and apply software testing and verification processes.
Knowledge and competence in software security and cyber defense.
Project-based application and presentation skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Fundamentals of Defense Systems Software
Hafta 3: Military Communication and Data Security
Hafta 6: Target Detection and Tracking Software
Hafta 7: Defense Systems Testing and Verification
Hafta 9: Automatic Control Systems and Software
Hafta 10: Artificial Intelligence in Defense Systems
Hafta 13: Maintenance and Support Software for Defense Systems
Hafta 14: Industry Standards for Defense Systems Software
resources: Defense Systems Software: Basics and Applications Prof.Dr. Giovanni Rossi and Dr.Maria Bianchi

courses.name: Electronic Warfare Flight Test
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain to students how the testing and evaluation of airborne electronic warfare systems can be conducted most effectively. It also aims to provide the fundamental knowledge and understanding needed for students to improve their skills in electronic warfare and succeed professionally.
courses.description: This course helps students explore how to conduct the testing and evaluation of airborne electronic warfare systems in the best way. The course covers threats and electronic warfare defense systems, the electronic warfare test process, test monitoring equipment and test facilities, the role of modeling and analysis, and the effects of signature technology. Students also examine case studies for electronic warfare test programs and gain expertise in development test and operational test industry requirements.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic concepts of electronic warfare systems.
Comprehend the testing and evaluation processes of electronic warfare defense systems.
Develop skills in planning and conducting test processes.
Use the monitoring equipment and test facilities required for electronic warfare flight testing.
Apply modeling and analysis techniques in electronic warfare flight tests.
Evaluate the effects of signature technology on electronic warfare systems.
Examine and analyze case studies for electronic warfare test programs.
Specialize in industry requirements for development test and operational test.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic Concepts of Electronic Warfare Systems
Hafta 2: Introduction to Electronic Warfare Defense Systems
Hafta 3: Planning and Organization of the Test Process
Hafta 4: Use of Test Monitoring Equipment
Hafta 5: Evaluation of Test Facilities and Infrastructure
Hafta 6: Application of Modeling and Simulation Techniques
Hafta 7: Analysis Methods and Data Evaluation
Hafta 8: Role of Signature Technology in Electronic Warfare Tests
Hafta 9: Safety and Risk Management in Electronic Warfare Flight Testing
Hafta 10: Case Studies of Electronic Warfare Test Programs
Hafta 11: Evaluation and Reporting of Test Results
Hafta 12: Industry Requirements for Development Test and Operational Test
Hafta 13: Ethical and Legal Issues in Electronic Warfare Flight Testing
Hafta 14: General Review of the Course and Future Planning
resources: Principles of Electronic Warfare by Christopher D. Davis
Electronic Warfare in the Information Age by David Adamy, 2015, Artech House Publishers
Electronic Warfare: Element of Strategy and Multiplier of Combat Power by Edward C. Ezell
Electronic Warfare and Radar Systems Engineering Handbook by United States Navy

courses.name: Bioinformatics Modeling and Simulation Techniques
courses.purpose: This course aims to apply data mining to the medical field. It includes mining clinical datasets typically found in patient medical records, image mining, medical mining, machine learning applied to data mining and general genomic data, and more. Different imaging techniques used to produce patient-specific models are used in computational fluid dynamics software to analyze fluid flow.
courses.description: After presenting the introductory material required in the field of modeling and simulation, the course moves on to modeling itself, including mathematical representations of human elements and the use of various modeling techniques designed to mimic humans and human systems.
learning_outcomes: Medical Decision Support Systems
Drug Design
Nano-drug Delivery Systems
Modeling of Human Systems
Humans as Models
Creating Successful Simulations in Healthcare
Robotics
Patient Care
Physical Modeling Techniques
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Application of probabilistic and related logics to medical decision support
Hafta 2: Particle dynamics and design of nano-drug delivery systems
Hafta 3: Drug design with machine learning and artificial neural networks
Hafta 10: Use of robotics: advantages and disadvantages
Hafta 11: Creating successful simulations in healthcare
resources: Computational Medicine in Data Mining and Modeling, Rakocevic, G., Djukic, T., Filipovic, N., Milutinovic, V., 2013, Springer
Course notes/textbooks

courses.name: Blockchain Technology
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a comprehensive overview of blockchain technology. It covers the technology's fundamental characteristics, philosophy, cryptographic foundations, consensus mechanisms and protocols, platforms, decentralized infrastructure, smart contracts, and the value and current applications of the technology in business.
courses.description: Introduction to blockchain. Fundamental features, philosophy and mechanisms. Blockchain platforms and ecosystems. Consensus mechanisms and protocols. Smart contracts and decentralized applications. Development tools and frameworks. Cryptographic foundations. Firm value and future expectations. Industrial applications. Barriers and challenges.
learning_outcomes: Students will have a holistic understanding of blockchain technology.
Students will gain in-depth knowledge of the technical aspects of blockchain technology.
Students will develop broad awareness of blockchain technology's firm value, industrial applications, and challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Fundamental features, philosophy and types
Hafta 4: Blockchain platforms and decentralized ecosystems
Hafta 5: Blockchain platforms and decentralized ecosystems continued
Hafta 10: Firm value and future expectations
Hafta 13: Future trends: toward a decentralized society

Daniel Drescher (2017). Blockchain Basics: A non-technical introduction in 25 steps. APress.

courses.name: Internet of Things Fundamentals
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a comprehensive treatment of the Internet of Things (IoT) philosophy, which is the prevailing approach in today's web-based, network-connected ecosystems. The goal is to present both the technical and business value of this revolutionary IT-based paradigm.
courses.description: Introduction to IoT. Fundamental features and philosophy. IoT architecture. Sensor-based networks. IoT communication technologies and connectivity. IoT components. Cloud computing. Edge and fog computing. Blockchain and smart contracts. Enterprise value and expectations: application integration and IoT-based analytics. Industry 4.0. Industrial applications. Barriers and challenges. Future trends: toward smart systems.
learning_outcomes: Have a general understanding of the IoT philosophy.
Have in-depth knowledge of the technical aspects of IoT.
Have broad awareness of enterprise value, industrial applications, and challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: IoT communication technologies and IoT connectivity
Hafta 6: IoT components- Cloud computing, Edge and fog computing, blockchain and smart contracts.
Hafta 11: Enterprise value and expectations: application integration and IoT-based analytics.
Hafta 12: Industry 4.0. Industrial applications. Barriers and challenges
Hafta 13: Future trends: toward smart systems

Building the Internet of Things: Implement New Business Models, Disrupt Competitors, Transform Your Industry - Maciej Kranz. Wiley, 2016.

courses.name: Introduction to Quantum Physics for Engineers
courses.purpose: To provide engineering students with a comprehensive understanding of the fundamentals of quantum physics and its applications in various engineering fields.
courses.description: This course, designed for students from different science and engineering backgrounds, covers the fundamental concepts of quantum physics for students in all engineering departments, examines the latest quantum technologies, and explores the challenges and future prospects in quantum engineering. It aims to provide a solid foundation in both the theoretical and practical aspects of quantum physics and equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of quantum technologies.
learning_outcomes: Understand and explain the fundamental concepts of quantum physics.
Apply mathematical tools and techniques to solve quantum physics problems.
Analyze and evaluate the potential impact of quantum technologies on various industries.
Gain knowledge about quantum technologies and their applications in engineering.
Communicate effectively about quantum physics concepts and their applications in engineering contexts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: An Overview of Quantum Technologies
Hafta 9: Quantum Lasers in Biomedical Engineering
Hafta 12: Applications of Quantum Materials and Devices
Hafta 13: Applications of Quantum Materials and Devices

courses.name: Database Systems
courses.purpose: To demonstrate the design and implementation of database management systems.
courses.description: Introduction to DBMS, conceptual, logical and physical databases, data modeling for a database. Structure of a DBMS. ERD model. Relational Model. Database Design, query languages. Real SQL programming.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts of the relational model, including schemas, and demonstrate the ability to use SQL for basic data retrieval.
Apply basic operations in relational algebra and understand algebraic manipulation of bags to perform effective data querying and manipulation.
Create and interpret entity-relationship (E/R) diagrams, handle weak entity sets, and demonstrate the ability to transform E/R diagrams into relational representations.
Use advanced SQL features to perform complex multi-relation queries and data modification operations by leveraging extended relational algebra, outer joins, and grouping/aggregation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Relational Model, Schemas, SQL
Hafta 2: Relational Algebra: Basic Operations, Join Algebra
Hafta 3: Relational Algebra: Basic Operations, Join Algebra
Hafta 4: Entity-Relationship Model: E/R Diagrams, Weak Entity Sets, Transforming E/R Diagrams into Relations
Hafta 5: Constraints: Foreign Keys, Local and General Constraints, Triggers
Hafta 6: Introduction to SQL: Select-From-Where Statements
Hafta 7: Introduction to SQL: Multiple-Relation Queries
Hafta 10: More SQL: Extended Relational Algebra, Outer Joins, Grouping/Aggregation, Insert/Delete/Update
Hafta 11: More SQL: Extended Relational Algebra, Outer Joins, Grouping/Aggregation, Insert/Delete/Update
Hafta 12: Constraints: Primary and Foreign Keys, Local and Global Constraints
Hafta 13: Constraints: Primary and Foreign Keys, Local and Global Constraints
resources: Database Systems: The Complete Book, Molina, Ullman, and Widom, 2/E, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Operating Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the theory of modern operating systems, including process and thread concepts, memory management, file systems, and input/output principles.
courses.description: Introduction to operating systems; processes and threads; process and thread scheduling; interprocess communication. Memory management; basic memory management techniques, virtual memory management technique, operating system policies for virtual memory management, and segmented memory structure. File systems; files, directories, implementation of file systems, and example file systems. Input/Output (I/O); I/O software and hardware principles, layered I/O structure, disks, clocks, terminals, and graphical interface units. Deadlock; resources, deadlock, deadlock detection and recovery, deadlock avoidance and prevention. Multimedia operating systems. Security. Distributed and network operating systems.
learning_outcomes: Provide a brief history of earlier operating systems and explain how modern operating systems work.
Define operating systems structurally.
Design core operating system components such as processes, threads, scheduling, and interprocess communication using semaphores, shared memory, and message passing techniques.
Evaluate the results of memory management, input/output techniques, and file systems.
Apply security and protection measures to a computer.
resources: Modern Operating Systems, Tanenbaum, 3/E, Prentice Hall

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with fundamental knowledge about probability.
courses.description: Mathematical probability. Conditional probability and independence. Basic probability models. Random variables; discrete random variables and their distributions. Continuous random variables and their distributions. Joint probability distributions. Expectation. Covariance and correlation.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic concepts of probability such as conditioning, independence, expectation, and variability.
Apply and interpret important theorems such as the law of total probability, Bayes' theorem, conditional expectation, the central limit theorem, and queueing theory.
Know when to use discrete and continuous distributions.
Model sources of uncertainty in problems related to industrial and systems engineering.
Analyze probabilistic models using probability theory and interpret the results obtained.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Sample space, event and set theory
Hafta 5: Probability, conditional probability and independence
Hafta 6: Random variables and their functions
Hafta 7: Discrete random variables and probability distributions
Hafta 9: Continuous random variables and probability distributions, joint probability distributions
Hafta 12: Expected value and conditional expectation
Hafta 13: Moments and moment generating functions
Hafta 15: Limit theorems and the central limit theorem
resources: W.W. Hines, D.C. Montgomery, D.M. Goldsman, C.M. Borror, Probability and Statistics in Engineering, 4th Ed., John Wiley & Sons, Inc.

Douglas C. Montgomery and George C. Runger, Applied Statistics and Probability for Engineers, 5th edition, Wiley

courses.name: İnsansız ve Otonom Araç Sistemleri
courses.purpose: Bu ders; İHA (UAV), İKA (UGV), AGV ve İDA (AUV) dahil olmak üzere insansız ve otonom sistemlerin temel ilkelerini tanıtır. Otonom platformlar için araç dinamiği, sensörler, kontrol, güdüm, seyrüsefer ve algılama tekniklerini kapsar. Öğrenciler; MATLAB, Simulink ve Python kullanarak modelleme, simülasyon ve analiz becerileri kazanarak otonom sistem bileşenlerini sanal bir ortamda uygulama ve entegre etme yetkinliği edinirler. Ders, otonominin pratik zorluklarını keşfetmek için uygulamalı simülasyon projelerine vurgu yapar. Dersin sonunda öğrenciler; otonom alt sistemleri analiz edebilecek, tasarlayabilecek, sergileyebilecek ve her bir bileşenin bilgi akışını ve işleyişini kavrayacaklardır.
courses.description: Otonom sistem bileşenleri, alt sistemlerin rolleri, kontrol sistemleri, güdüm algoritmaları, seyrüsefer sistemleri, sensörler, yol planlama ve algılama ile iletişim protokolleri.
learning_outcomes: İHA (UAV), İKA (UGV), AGV ve İDA (AUV) dahil olmak üzere insansız ve otonom sistemlerin ilkeleri ve bileşenleri hakkında bilgi sahibi olduğunu gösterir.
Gezgin bir robotun dinamik modellemesini anlar.
Sanal bir ortamda kontrol, güdüm ve seyrüsefer algoritmaları tasarlar ve uygular.
Otonom sistem karar verme süreçleri için çoklu sensör füzyonu ve algılama tekniklerini uygular.
Koordinatlı görevleri yerine getirmek ve sistem performansını analiz etmek için otonom alt sistemleri simülasyona entegre eder.

Hafta 9: Çoklu Sensör Ortamlarında Veri Füzyonu

Hafta 13: Otonomi için Algılama ve Bilgisayarlı Görü
Hafta 15: MATLAB/Simulink ile Otonom Sistemlerin Simülasyonu

courses.name: Management Information Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of management information systems concepts and the reflections of related applications in business life.
courses.description: Ethical and social issues in information systems, telecommunications, internet and wireless technologies, information systems security, e-commerce: digital markets and digital products, and knowledge management.
learning_outcomes: Understands the difficulties that may arise during the planning, structuring, and management of information systems.
Thinks about the performance of information technology.
Evaluates the role of major system types in an organization and their relationships with each other.

Hafta 17: Student Presentations and Final Exam

courses.name: Decision Theory
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students learn fundamental decision-making concepts and tools, and to teach appropriate methods that enable them to model problems so they can analyze complex problems.
courses.description: Elements of decision problems, modeling decisions, evaluating alternatives, sensitivity analysis, decision problems and uncertainty, subjective probability, risk behavior, multi-criteria decision-making methods (AHP and TOPSIS), utility theory, and multi-objective decision making.
learning_outcomes: Analyze, formulate, and solve decision problems.
Use modeling techniques for decision problems.
Analyze and solve decision problems under uncertainty and risk.
Use software for decision analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Elements of decision problems, modeling decisions
Hafta 11: Multi-Criteria Decision Making (AHP and TOPSIS)
resources: R. T. Clemen, T. Reilly, Making Hard Decisions with Decision Tools, Duxbury, 2001.
H. A. Taha, Operations Research: An Introduction, Eighth Edition, Prentice-Hall, 2007.

courses.name: Fuzzy Logic and Modeling
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı belirsizliğin bulanık mantıkla modellenmesidir. Bu konseptte bulanık kümeler, bulanık sayı ve bulanık aritmetiğin yanı sıra Zadeh'in Genişleme Prensibi de tanıtılmaktadır. Ayrıca bulanık kümeleme algoritmaları kullanılarak modellemenin nasıl yapılacağını öğrenmek, bulanık fonksiyonların geliştirilmesi ve bulanık fonksiyon yaklaşımının tartışılması da bu dersin amacıdır.
courses.description: Bulanık Kümeler, Bulanık Mantık, Bulanık İlişkiler, Üyelik fonksiyonları, Berraklaştırma, Bulanık kural tabanlı sistem, Bulanık Karar verme.

Bulanık üyelik Fonksiyonlarının kullanımını anlar.
Klasikilişkiler ile bulanık ilişkilerin farkını anlar.
Berraklaştırma yöntemlerini öğrenir ve ardından bunu sistemin bulanık çıktısını berraklaştırmak için kullanır.
Bulanık Çıkarım Sistemini (FIS) uygular.

courses.name: Sezgisel Algoritmalar
courses.purpose: Çeşitli optimizasyon problemlerinin çözümünde sezgisel algoritmaların öğrenilmesi, sınıflandırılması ve karşılaştırılması.
courses.description: Zor kombinatoryal problemlerin çözümü için sezgisel arama yaklaşımları. Sezgisel arama tekniklerinin sınıflandırılması. Kombinatoryal en iyileme problemleri için geleneksel sezgisel yöntemler. Tavlama benzetimi, tabu arama ve evrimsel algoritmaları içeren meta sezgisel yöntemler. Kısıt işleme teknikleri.
learning_outcomes: Kesin sonuçlu algoritmalar, yaklaşım algoritmaları ve sezgisel algoritmalar arasındaki farkın açıklanması
Geleneksel yapı ve iyileştirme sezgisel algoritmalarının temel türlerini öğrenilmesi
Tavlama benzetimi, tabu arama, evrimsel algoritmalar ve sürü zekası gibi meta sezgisellerin temel ilkelerini ve operatörlerinin öğrenilmesi.
Seçtikleri bir optimizasyon problemi için bir meta-sezgisel arama algoritması geliştirilmesi ve uygulanması.
Parametre ayarlama ile ilgili zorlukları ve bunların nasıl ele alınabileceğinin öğrenilmesi.

courses.name: Machine Learning in Combinatorial Optimization
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with a comprehensive understanding of machine learning techniques and their effective integration with combinatorial optimization problems. Students will learn to design, analyze, and improve optimization processes, gaining the ability to enhance efficiency, quality, and overall performance. The course also covers key topics such as optimization strategy, process management, quality control, supply chain management, forecasting methods, project management, and lean systems.
courses.description: This course introduces the fundamental concepts, applications, and advanced methods of combining machine learning with combinatorial optimization. It is designed to help students develop a deep understanding of both fields and prepare them to solve complex optimization problems across different domains using current tools and techniques.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts of machine learning and combinatorial optimization.
Examine various machine learning techniques for solving combinatorial optimization problems.
Gain hands-on experience with relevant tools and libraries.
Develop the ability to apply ML methods to CO problems and critically evaluate the results.

courses.name: Internship II
courses.purpose: Students will integrate their academic studies with industry experience during their internship. The course helps students understand workplace environments and organizational processes in industry and develop a vision for the fields in which they may wish to specialize.
courses.description: This internship course provides a four-week (20 working days) field experience in a workplace relevant to Software Engineering, in the public or private sector. Students are required to follow the procedures in the THKU Internship Guide in order to complete the internship successfully.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to evaluate the practical reflections of the theoretical knowledge gained in university education.
Will be able to generate alternatives individually or as a team to solve problems encountered in practice.
Will be able to present the acquired knowledge in an official report.
Will be able to define the relationships between units in the institution/company where the internship is carried out.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Professional experience is expected to be gained.
Hafta 2: Observation of the institution's various activities and operating rules is expected.
Hafta 3: Active participation in the institution's activities is expected as much as possible.

courses.name: Sistem Geliştirme İçin Veri Modelleme
courses.purpose: Veri analizi ve modelleme tekniklerini öğrenmek ve uygulayabilmek. Ders kapsamında ele alınacak konular, akademik çalışma ve uygulama alanlarında dünya çapında kullanılmaktadır. Bu dersi alacak öğrenciler, ders kapsamında anlatılacak yöntem ve modelleri kullanarak akademik ve uygulama alanlarında büyük avantaj sağlayabilecektir.

Model kavramını bilir (Simülasyon ve öngörü).

courses.name: Requirements Definition and Analysis
courses.purpose: This course provides a foundation for carrying out all activities related to information systems development from requirements analysis to system design, from the beginning to the end.
courses.description: System development environment, sources of software, information systems project management, system planning and selection, determining system requirements, structuring system requirements: process modeling and conceptual data modeling, interface design, database design, system implementation and operation, object-oriented analysis and design, agile methodologies.
learning_outcomes: Develop software
Test software
Maintain software
Determine and analyze requirements
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Managing an Information Systems Project
Hafta 4: Managing an Information Systems Project
Hafta 7: Structuring System Requirements: Process Modeling
Hafta 8: Structuring System Requirements: Process Modeling
Hafta 9: Structuring System Requirements: Conceptual Data Modeling
Hafta 10: Structuring System Requirements: Conceptual Data Modeling
resources: Joseph S. Valacich, Joey F. George, Jeffrey A. Hoffer (2012) Essentials of Systems Analysis and Design, Fifth Edition, Pearson.
Kendall K.E. and Kendall J.E. (2011) Systems Analysis & Design, 8th edition, Pearson.
Haluk Kul, Bilişim Sistemleri Temelleri ve Uygulamaları, Papatya Yayıncılık, İstanbul.
Ders Notları (Selçuk ALP).
Erhan Sarıdoğan, Yazılım Mühendisliği Temelleri, Papatya Yayıncılık, İstanbul, 2011.
